Production - Agent Companion - Skills loaded incorrectly into Claude SDK (wrong path, combined file, broken frontmatter)
bugs.description:
Bug DescriptionThe Agent Companion skill loading implementation does not follow the Claude Agent SDK standard for skills. This causes skills to be discovered incorrectly, shows internal server filesystem paths to users in the UI, and potentially loads wrong/stale skills across agents.Root Cause AnalysisThree separate issues were identified in backend/services/agent_companion/claude/config.py (_handle_skill_file function):1. All skills combined into a single SKILL.md file (breaks SDK discovery)The current code loads all skills from S3 and concatenates them with --- separators into one SKILL.md file:combined_skills = '\n\n---\n\n'.join(all_skills_content)Problem: The Claude SDK expects each skill in its own directory (~/.claude/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md), each with its own YAML frontmatter containing name and description. The --- separator is the YAML frontmatter delimiter, so combining skills this way destroys the YAML frontmatter of all skills except the first one. The SDK sees one mega-skill instead of individual skills.SDK Standard: Each skill must be in its own directory:.claude/skills/
  data-visualization/
    SKILL.md
  data-querying/
    SKILL.md2. Path mismatch between skill write path and SDK cwd (skills not found or wrong skills loaded)_handle_skill_file writes skills using os.path.realpath(project_name) where project_name is the agent name (e.g., snowflake-oauth), producing a path like: ~/.claude/skills/-app-backend-snowflake-oauth/SKILL.mdBut create_claude_options sets the SDK's cwd to project_name which is assistant_ui (a different variable). The SDK discovers project-level skills relative to cwd, so these two paths never match.This is also environment-dependent: os.path.realpath('snowflake-oauth') produces completely different paths on local dev vs Docker container vs ECS.3. Cross-agent skill contaminationSkills are written to ~/.claude/skills/ (user-level) and setting_sources includes user. User-level skills are global -- the SDK loads ALL user skills regardless of which agent is active. Agent A's skills are visible to Agent B.ImpactUsers see internal server filesystem paths (e.g., /home/appuser/.claude/skills/-app-backend-snowflake-oauth) in the chat UI when skills are invokedSkills may not load at all due to path mismatchSkills from one agent may leak into another agent's sessionOnly the first skill's YAML frontmatter is visible to Claude for auto-discoveryAll skill content loads at once instead of lazily per-skillFix Plan (Option 1: Project-scoped skills)Deterministic per-agent cwd: Use ~/.claude/agent-workspaces/{agent_project_name} as the SDK cwdOne directory per skill: Write each S3 skill to {cwd}/.claude/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md with original YAML frontmatter intactProject-only setting source: Change setting_sources from ['user', 'project'] to ['project']Stale skill cleanup: Before writing, remove old skill directories not in current skills-config.jsonFiles to Changebackend/services/agent_companion/claude/config.py - Rewrite _handle_skill_file, fix cwd in create_claude_optionsbackend/services/agent_companion/claude/message_processor.py - Verify SKILL_CONTENT_INDICATORS still match new paths.cursor/skills/agent-companion-structure/SKILL.md - Update documentationEnvironmentAffects both dev and production (Docker container running as appuser with CWD /app/backend).

Production - Bug in Workato Function: AI Notion
bugs.description:
The current Workato recipe logic contains a data mapping error in the Update Issue in Jira step. The "Notion Link" field is currently hardcoded to pull the URL datapill exclusively from Step 17 (the "IF" branch).

When the recipe logic follows the "ELSE" branch (Step 19), Step 17 is skipped. Consequently, the datapill from Step 17 remains null, causing the Jira update to either fail or post an empty link.

bugs.summary: Production - Error found in Auto Provisioning process in NetSuite
bugs.description: Automated role provisioning is currently failing on Employee records due to two distinct script errors in the handleProvisioning function of the HBN.UE.Entity.Form.Admin.js script. These errors prevent the correct assignment/revocation of roles and impact the RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) automation.Error Breakdown1. Invalid Search Column (SSS_INVALID_SRCH_COL)Occurrence: 15/01/2026 9:05 amLocation: getRoleMatrix function (line 228)Root Cause: The search is attempting to reference a column custrecord_hb_pm_bob_sub_department that either does not exist on the custom record customrecord_hb_provisioning_matrix or is misspelled.Evidence:"message": "An nlobjSearchColumn contains an invalid column... custrecord_hb_pm_bob_sub_department."2. Invalid API Usage (SSS_INVALID_API_USAGE)Occurrence: 14/01/2026 7:05 amLocation: handleProvisioning function (line 94)Root Cause: The script is calling rec.getText() on a field that has just been modified via rec.setValue() within the same beforeSubmit execution. In NetSuite, when a value is set in memory during beforeSubmit, you must use getValue to retrieve it, as the "text" (display name) has not yet been committed to the database.Evidence:"message": "Invalid API usage. You must use getValue to return the value set with setValue."

bugs.summary: Production - Issue with Dealhub google sheet uplaod
Refactor  Account Scheduler Daily Flow + No Activity issue
bugs.description: 🧾 DescriptionThe Account Scheduler Flow runs daily and includes several processes, one of which is intended to revert prospect accounts (in the SMB and Micro segments) to "nurture" if there has been no activity within the last 60 days from the MQA date. This specific process is currently not functioning as expected, Accounts meeting the criteria are not being reverted.In addition, the flow still contains logic referencing the 6sense integration, which is no longer in use. As 6sense has been sunset, all related processes must be removed from the scheduler.💼 Business ImpactThe failure of this process leads to stale prospect accounts remaining in active status, affecting sales prioritization, outreach effectiveness, and reporting accuracy. Retaining deprecated 6sense logic adds unnecessary complexity and potential confusion for admins maintaining the flow.🔍 Expected BehaviorThe process within the Account Scheduler Flow should correctly identify prospect accounts in the SMB and Micro segments that have had no activity for 60 days since the MQA date and revert them to "nurture" status.All processes or conditions referencing the 6sense integration should be fully removed from the flow.🛠️ Suggested FixUpdate entry criteria: Remove all 6sense references and ensure the scheduler flow still supports all remaining processes.Remove 6sense outcomes: Delete any flow logic, branches, or actions tied to 6sense.Fix “No activity in last 60 days” process: Adjust its entry criteria and ensure the process executes correctly for SMB and Micro for prospect accounts.Test: Validate changes with sample accounts to confirm expected behavior.

bugs.summary: Production - Sandbox module charges cancelled due to incorrect negative usage line on invoice INVNL31525
bugs.description: Email address:noam.muskat@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Sandbox module charges cancelled due to incorrect negative usage line on invoice INVNL31525Your Department:FinancePlease specify your team:Billing & CollectionFinance Area:BillingBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:HighRegression:Not SureSystems Involved:NetsuiteDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:1. Open invoice INVNL31525 in the approval queue.2. Review the Sandbox module section in the invoice preview.3. Observe that three Sandbox-related lines appear:Two regular charge linesOne additional line displayed in a separate table below the charge lines4. Note that the additional line displays the full module usage as a negative amount.Expected Behavior:The Sandbox module should bill correctly based on the active charge line CL-0075406 (effective from 21/07/2025), as it did in the previous month.Actual Behavior:A separate Sandbox usage line appears in the preview with the entire usage shown as a negative value, which cancels out the module charges completely.As a result, the invoice shows zero billing for Sandbox instead of the correct charge.Is there an available workaround?:NoPlease provide any relevant links:https://4672400.app.netsuite.com/app/accounting/transactions/custinvc.nl?id=9043834———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: Production - fact_bob_jobs - BUG
bugs.description:
PROD_DWH.HR_FPNA.Fact_bob_jobs – The column JOB_CODE includes an incorrect code for employees who have multiple codes in their job field. Please use only the value in the last set of brackets.
For example:
For the value "Executives (C level) (11) Chief Information Officer Executive MIS (J-3082954994)", the JOB_CODE should be J-3082954994, not 11 (as it is currently).

option of solution:
with final as (
    select wh.employee_id,
            json_data:"history":"effectiveDate"::date as effective_date,
            json_data:"history":"employee":"jobProfileId":"historicalChangedBy"::varchar as changed_by_id,
--- NEW FROM HERE-----
reverse(
    regexp_substr(
        reverse(json_data:"humanReadable":"history":"employee":"jobProfileId":"historicalValue"),
        '\\)([^()]*)\\(',1,1,'e')
--- NEW END ------
) AS job_code,            json_data:"humanReadable":"history":"work":"title":"historicalValue"::varchar as title,
            jp.job_profile_id,
            jp.job_title,
            jp.job_status,
            jp.job_role,
            jp.job_role_id,
            jp.job_family_group,
            jp.job_family_group_id,
            jp.job_family,
            jp.job_family_id,
            jp.job_level_role,
            jp.job_level_role_id,
            jp.job_function,

    convert_timezone('UTC', current_timestamp) as dwh_inserted_at,
    convert_timezone('UTC', current_timestamp) as dwh_updated_at

    from prod_datalake_db.bob_api.employee_job_work_history wh
        left join PROD_DWH.hr_fpna.dim_bob_job_profiles jp
            on job_code = jp.job_profile_code
)

select *
from final

bugs.summary: Production - Production - WTFL - forecast - update PREVIOUS_IS_DUMMY_CORE_forecast logic
bugs.description: need to update the logic of PREVIOUS_IS_DUMMY_CORE_forecast - as today it’s cousing to wrong bucket.current logic we asked in STT: where model_type ='forecast' -check the previous day:If previous day we has 'forecast' record take the forcast record and calculate the lag:lag(Is_Dummy_Core) over (partition by revenue_type,model_type, company_id, product_code order by date_id)Else take the actual record and calculate the lag:lag(Is_Dummy_Core) over (partition by revenue_type, company_id, product_code order by date_id)need to be changed to → when model_type ='forecast' -check the previous day:If previous day we have 'actual' record take the actual record and calculate the lag:lag(Is_Dummy_Core) over (partition by revenue_type, company_id, product_code order by date_id)If previous day we have 'forecast' and there was no 'actual' record for that partition,then take the forcast record and calculate the lag:lag(Is_Dummy_Core) over (partition by revenue_type,model_type, company_id, product_code order by date_id)It’s actually to change the order - so in case of model_type='forecast'  when we have a record for the same company, same revenue type, same date and same product → a record in model_type ='actual' in the previous day → then the is_dummy_core of the previous day should be taken from the actual record.in case we of model_type='forecast' but there’s no record of ‘actual’ on the previuos day for the partition - then the is_dummy_core of the previous day for that record should be taken from the ‘forecast’ record

bugs.summary: Production - Agent Service Bugs
bugs.description:
Bug: Missing Agent Records & Incorrect Error Mapping in ICP OrchestratorSession UUID: 88607fd5-51a3-4cdb-a92e-5b07a8f35e0eRelated Job: https://app.workato.com/recipes/63347553/job/j-AXCrM8od-wLfADpSummaryA job failed with a 400 error code due to a Website Scraping Agent failure. Investigation shows missing agent records, incorrect agent-to-status mapping, and an incorrect error message returned by the GAI service logs.Issues Identified1. Missing Agents in agent_status.jsonThe agent_status.json file for the session is missing two expected agents:Tech StackGoogle PageSpeedThese should appear for every modern ICP run but are absent for this session.2. Missing Records in TBL_ORCHESTRATION_AGENT_LOGSThe table PROD_INTEGRATION.ADMINISTRATION.TBL_ORCHESTRATION_AGENT_LOGS contains 13 records for the provided session UUID.However, the Tech Stack agent record is missing, even though it should be logged like the others.3. Incorrect Mapping of Failed AgentIn TBL_ORCHESTRATION_AGENT_LOGS, the failed agent “Website Scraping” is incorrectly mapped to the value "Unknown" instead of the correct agent name.4. Incorrect Error Message in GAI_AGENT_SERVICE_RUN_LOGSThe table PROD_INTEGRATION.ADMINISTRATION.GAI_AGENT_SERVICE_RUN_LOGS (expected to show only the orchestrator-level agent execution) does not display the actual error.Instead of the expected message, it shows:"too many values to unpack (expected 2)"Expected error message:Website Scraping Agent failed with error:
Mandatory tool Get Website Content failure:
{'message': "HTTPSConnectionPool(host='recrutement-expertimo.fr', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=10)"}
with status code: 400
Expected BehaviorAll agents (including Tech Stack and Google PageSpeed) should appear in agent_status.json.TBL_ORCHESTRATION_AGENT_LOGS should contain records for all agents and correctly map failed agents to their proper names.GAI_AGENT_SERVICE_RUN_LOGS should return the full underlying error instead of a Python unpacking error.ImpactDebugging becomes significantly harder due to missing and incorrect metadata.Monitoring and alerting systems relying on proper agent logs may misfire or miss failures.Some agents may not be executed or tracked correctly during ICP runs.

bugs.summary: Production - CW Time Stamp is Missing in Auto Renewals
bugs.description:
Almost 80 auto renewals are missing the CW time stamp. The reason is the the opp fields assignment was changed to work on before action, and since the auto renewal closes with autolaunch flow which is triggered by workato field update, salesforce consider the closer as the same transaction and not triggering the before flow.

Solution: move the CW fields assignment to the auto renewal closure autolaunch flow.QA was done in this Opp:

bugs.summary: Production - Billing contact removed in SF still receives invoices in NS after renewal (sync issue between SF and NS)
bugs.description: Email address:noam.muskat@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Billing contact removed in SF still receives invoices in NS after renewal (sync issue between SF and NS)Your Department:FinancePlease specify your team:Billing & CollectionFinance Area:BillingBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:CriticalRegression:Not SureSystems Involved:NetsuiteDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:1. Go through a customer renewal flow in SF2. In the renewal contract, set a new billing contact that is different from the existing primary billing contact in NS3. SF automatically marks the old billing contact as “remove from NS"4. In NS, open the customer record and observe:The old primary contact appears as a separate line without a roleThe new billing contact appears correctly as the primary one4. Check the customer’s Communication tab in NSExpected Behavior:Only the new billing contact defined in the SF renewal should receive invoices.The old billing contact marked in SF as “remove from NS” should no longer appear in NS or receive any invoice communications.The updated contact information should sync before the invoice is sent after the renewal.Actual Behavior:Although SF marks the old billing contact as “remove from NS,” NS still sends invoices to both:the new billing contact (correct)the old billing contact (incorrect), which still appears in NS without a role.This happens even though the update was completed prior to the invoice being issued.Examples:1. https://4672400.app.netsuite.com/app/common/entity/custjob.nl?id=4962342. https://4672400.app.netsuite.com/app/common/entity/custjob.nl?id=139093. https://4672400.app.netsuite.com/app/common/entity/custjob.nl?id=820585Is there an available workaround?:No———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: Production - Account that comes back from nurtre after Follow up date arrives- gets wrong MQA origin
bugs.description:
In specific cases where the AE nurtured the account after  CL of an opp and added a follow up date to get the account back after x days:
We restore the last MQA origin.
In this example - https://hibob.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/0011v00002o3fyZAAQ/related/Histories/view
The original MQA origin was a "Named Account BDR" but the owner assigned was the AE  (cause he was the opp owner).
When we restore from an opp that was CL, we need to make sure that if the restored MQA origin is a "Named Account BDR", it should change to align with the AE/AAE role. E.g we need to swap it to a "Named Account AE'Flow to check:Account Scheduler - Global ABX Status Change Scheduler Daily FlowCopado Package:BT-8601 Remove Partner Exclusion from 60 days process & BT-8789Tech Notes:Added 2 decision outcomes in the ‘bdr is active’ element with respective 2 Updated Records elements to update name account AE/AAE

bugs.summary: Production - License Deactivation in CaptivateIQ not working automatically after employee termination (licenses not being released).
bugs.description: Email address:alexandra.nikitin@hibob.ioIssue Summary:License Deactivation in CaptivateIQ not working automatically after employee termination (licenses not being released).Your Department:FinancePlease specify your team:FinOps (Tech)Finance Area:CommissionsBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:MediumRegression:YesSystems Involved:CaptivateIQDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:When an employee is terminated, CaptivateIQ should automatically deactivate the user license 2 months after the termination date, following the final payout period.Currently, this deactivation process is not working — terminated users remain active beyond the 2-month window.As a result, over 80 licenses are still occupied by inactive users, leaving only 2 free licenses available.This issue blocks the onboarding of new joiners and urgently needs to be fixed.Expected Behavior:After termination, the employee’s license remains active only for 2 months (to cover final payout calculations).After the 2-month period, the user’s license should be automatically deactivated and released back to the license pool.Actual Behavior:Terminated users remain active indefinitely in CaptivateIQ.Licenses are not freed automatically.License count nearly exhausted — only 2 free licenses remain.Is there an available workaround?:NoPlease provide any relevant links:https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1kW-j4JHQ9puWRYiCV33tNrciVzmhTlXrBlvw7BtmP2Y/edit?gid=0#gid=0———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: Production - WTFL Forecast - END with value in case of Core_Dummy
bugs.description:
END for Core Dummy should be = 0;
see below where it's not the case:
select
    COMPANY_ID,
    PRODUCT_CODE,
    DATE_ID,
    MODEL_TYPE,
    REVENUE_TYPE,
    nvl(WEIGTHED_AVERAGE_ARPU,0) WEIGTHED_AVERAGE_ARPU,
    nvl(NUMBER_OF_EMPLOYEES,0) NUMBER_OF_EMPLOYEES,
    round(WEIGTHED_AVERAGE_ARPU * NUMBER_OF_EMPLOYEES*12) end_calc,
    end,
    "START",
    NEW_BUSINESS,
    UPSELL,
    UPLIFT,
    DOWNSELL,
    DOWNLIFT,
    churn,
    NATURAL_GROWTH,
    sum_of_buckets
    from dev_DWH.WATERFALL.MRT_WTFL_DAILY
where round(WEIGTHED_AVERAGE_ARPU * NUMBER_OF_EMPLOYEES*12) <> round(END)
  and abs ( end_calc - end) >1
and IS_ACTIVE_PRODUCT = true
--and COMPANY_ID='640357'
--and DATE_ID='2027-02-10'
and PRODUCT_CODE not like '%UKP%'
and is_dummy_core=true
--and MODEL_TYPE='actual'
--and DATE_ID <> CHURN_EXPECTED_DATE
order by COMPANY_ID, PRODUCT_CODE asc

bugs.summary: Production - Error in Github : PR Request - case sensitive query
bugs.description:
There is an error on recipe 'Github : PR Request' where a SuiteQL query is executed in step 35.
That expression assumes the list of objects (Rows) has a field called Name (capital N).
Workato’s formula engine is case-sensitive, so {Name} and {name} are not the same key.

bugs.summary: Production - PROD_DATALAKE_DB.SALESFORCE.OPPORTUNITY_LINE_ITEM  is not updated in the frequency it should which causes unexpected and unwanted behaviour in our SFDC production
bugs.description: Email address:adi.faians@hibob.ioIssue Summary:PROD_DATALAKE_DB.SALESFORCE.OPPORTUNITY_LINE_ITEM  is not updated in the frequency it should which causes unexpected and unwanted behaviour in our SFDC productionYour Department:MISBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:CriticalRegression:Not SureSystems Involved:SFDCDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:YesPlease specify the project \ process:Pulling CARR data to production SFDCSteps to Reproduce:We have a process in Workato that pulls data from the table PROD_DWH_DB.REPORTS.rpt_finance__waterall_opportunity_product_c and performs a join with the table PROD_DATALAKE_DB.SALESFORCE.OPPORTUNITY_LINE_ITEM to identify discrepancies and update deltas.Long story short — I’ve noticed that the table PROD_DATALAKE_DB.SALESFORCE.OPPORTUNITY_LINE_ITEM is not fully aligned with what I’m seeing in Salesforce for the same opportunity.For example, the following query:SELECT i.OPPORTUNITY_STAGE_C  FROM PROD_DATALAKE_DB.SALESFORCE.OPPORTUNITY_LINE_ITEM AS i  WHERE i.id = '00kVg00000Hd5sWIAR'Currently returns "Evaluating", whereas in Salesforce, as of 2:00 PM  on Nov 3rd, the stage was updated to "Closed Lost".almost 24 hrs later the data hasnt been refreshed when it should be near realtime This discrepancy is causing data issues and a domino effect of problems in my SFDc production.Expected Behavior:PROD_DATALAKE_DB.SALESFORCE.OPPORTUNITY_LINE_ITEM is updated every several minutes like it should.Actual Behavior:Table is stale. No update in the last 24 hrs.Is there an available workaround?:No———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.description:
for model type = actual
and following is not null - and there is no one month gap ( following vs end date) and this is not downsell (use case 3 ) - the pizuz need to be till the following start date or the churn date - earlier then the 2
--- today in the pizuz , if the terminate clone date is eralier then we have pizuz till the terminate - and in sone case we lose dates , need to remove terminate from the condition

example 
select COMPANY_ID,
       MODEL_TYPE,
             ACCOUNT_NAME,
             ACCOUNT_ID,
             OPPORTUNITY_TYPE,
             OPPORTUNITY_ID,
             OPPORTUNITY_LINE_ID,
             FOLLOWING_OPPORTUNITY_ID,
             PREVIOUS_OPPORTUNITY_ID,
             FOLLOWING_OPPORTUNITY_CARR_START_DATE,
             --FOLLOWING_OPP_ARR_FORECAST,
             FOLLOWING_OPPORTUNITY_CARR_START_DATE,
             PRODUCT_CODE,
             ORIG_CLOSE_WON_TIME_STAMP,
             CLOSE_WON_TIME_STAMP,
             CLOSE_DATE,
             OPPORTUNITY_PRODUCT_START_DATE,
             OPPORTUNITY_PRODUCT_END_DATE,
             RENEWAL_LINE_TYPE,
             NET_PRICE,

bugs.description: Email address:alexandra.oneill@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Missing Key Fields on Upsell Product Lines Due to Expired Product Close DatesYour Department:MISBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:CriticalRegression:NoSystems Involved:SFDCDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:Open an Upsell Opportunity that is over one year old (where the Product Close Date has passed).Review the Product Lines linked to that opportunity.Observe that the following fields are blank or not populating:WTRF CARRRevenue TypeProduct Line OwnerExpected Behavior:The Close Date field on the product should be in the future.Option 1: Then the 3 fields mentioned should populate correctly within 12 hours on Upsell Product LinesOption 2: Even if the opportunity is over a year old to ensure ARR is reflected accurately in pipeline and forecasting tools we should still calculate and pull in WTRF CARR & Rev Type.With these populated the opportunity should remain visible to the Account Manager in their pipeline management dashboards and reports in Clari.Actual Behavior:When the Product Close Date is expired (over a year old), the WTRF CARR, Revenue Type, and Product Line Owner fields fail to populate.As a result:ARR does not appear in the pipeline.The AM cannot see the opportunity in their management tool.Forecasts are missing these deals, impacting visibility and accuracy.Is there an available workaround?:NoPlease provide any relevant links:https://hibob.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Opportunity/006Vg00000EgAPVIA3/related/OpportunityLineItems/view———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: Production - Update vw_active_billed_employees_360_view
bugs.description:
The view had an issue and returned records with invalid SFDC ids.  old queryCREATE OR REPLACE VIEW PROD_INTEGRATION.DATA_ENRICHMENT.vw_active_billed_employees AS
WITH temp_billed_ee AS (SELECT C.EXTERNALID                                     AS Cust_SFDC,
                      C.CUSTENTITY_HB_BOB_ID_NUM                       AS BOB_ID,
                      C.COMPANYNAME                                    AS Customer_Name,
                      C.ID                                             AS NS_ID,
                      TL.CUSTCOL_HB_ZAB_START_DATE :: DATE             AS Tran_Date,
                      EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TL.CUSTCOL_HB_ZAB_START_DATE)  AS Year,
                      EXTRACT(MONTH FROM TL.CUSTCOL_HB_ZAB_START_DATE) AS Month,
                      ITM.FULLNAME,
                      SUM(TL.QUANTITY * -1)                            AS Quantity_billed
               FROM PROD_DATALAKE_DB.NETSUITE.TRANSACTION T
                        INNER JOIN PROD_DATALAKE_DB.NETSUITE.CUSTOMER C
                                   ON C.ID = T.ENTITY
                        INNER JOIN PROD_DATALAKE_DB.NETSUITE.TRANSACTIONLINE TL
                                   ON TL.TRANSACTION = T.ID AND MAINLINE = 'F' AND TAXLINE = 'F'
                        INNER JOIN PROD_DATALAKE_DB.NETSUITE.ITEM ITM
                                   ON ITM.ID = TL.ITEM AND ITM.ID = 681
               WHERE TYPE in ('CustInvc', 'CustCred')
                 AND CUSTBODY_HB_IMPLEMENTATION_INVOICE <> 'T'
                 AND TL.CUSTCOL_HB_ZAB_START_DATE IS NOT NULL
                 and T.POSTING = 'T'
                 and T._FIVETRAN_DELETED = false
                 and Tl._FIVETRAN_DELETED = false
                 and current_date :: DATE >= Tran_Date
               GROUP BY C.EXTERNALID,
                        C.CUSTENTITY_HB_BOB_ID_NUM,
                        C.COMPANYNAME,
                        C.ID,
                        TL.CUSTCOL_HB_ZAB_START_DATE,
                        ITM.FULLNAME)
SELECT
    bil.*,
    stat.ACTIVE_EMPLOYEES
FROM
    temp_billed_ee bil
INNER JOIN
    PROD_DATALAKE_DB.BOB_HIBOB_PUBLIC.COMPANY_STATS stat
    ON stat.COMPANY_ID::varchar = bil.BOB_ID::varchar AND bil.Tran_Date = stat.UPDATE_DATE;

bugs.summary: Production - Wrong customers numeric numbers
bugs.description: Bug Description: Wrong Customers Numeric NumbersSummaryThe customer numbering logic in NetSuite is incorrectly assigning child customer IDs when both the parent and child have regular (non-special) Bob IDs. Instead of assigning the next available sequential number, the system is appending a suffix (e.g., ":1") to the parent’s entity ID.Expected BehaviorWhen a new customer is created:If no parent is assigned: The script should assign the next available sequential numeric customer ID (e.g., if the highest is 34545, the next is 34546).If a parent is assigned:If both parent and child have regular Bob IDs (not special IDs like "PC:XXXXXX" or "99999999*"), the child should also receive the next available sequential number, not a suffixed ID.Only if the parent has a special ID should the child receive a suffixed ID (e.g., 34545:1).Actual BehaviorWhen a child customer is created under a parent with a regular Bob ID, the script assigns an ID with a suffix (e.g., 34545:1) instead of the next sequential number.Example:Parent: 34545 – Stuff LimitedChild: Stuff Digital Limited (should be assigned next sequential number, but receives 34545:1)ImpactThis misnumbering breaks the intended customer ID logic and causes confusion in customer hierarchy and reporting.There are already multiple tickets related to this issue.ReferencesExample ParentExample ChildSteps to ReproduceCreate a new customer in NetSuite and assign a parent customer with a regular Bob ID.Observe the assigned customer ID for the new child.Note that the ID is suffixed (e.g., 34545:1) instead of being the next available sequential number.Additional NotesThe logic should be reviewed and corrected to ensure that only customers under special parent IDs receive suffixed IDs.Please coordinate with the relevant stakeholders to prevent recurrence.

bugs.summary: Production - waterfall pizuz need to take into account also terminate & clone date
bugs.description:
we have cases that there is terminate and clone date - but the pizuz last after

the logic expected to be 
if there is no 'Following_Opportunity_ID' then bring the current opportunity ' least(coales (TERMINATE_AND_CLONE_DATE,OPPORTUNITY_PRODUCT_END_DATE) , CHURN_EFFECTIVE_DATE) );

example
select
    COMPANY_ID,
    DATE_ID,
       ACCOUNT_ID,
       REVENUE_TYPE,
       OPPORTUNITY_ID,
       FOLLOWING_OPPORTUNITY_ID,
       FOLLOWING_OPPORTUNITY_CARR_START_DATE,
       PRODUCT_CODE,
       CLOSE_WON_TIME_STAMP,
       CLOSE_DATE,
       OPPORTUNITY_PRODUCT_START_DATE,
       OPPORTUNITY_PRODUCT_END_DATE,
       RENEWAL_LINE_TYPE,
       NET_PRICE,
       USAGE_IND,
       ACTIVE_EES,
       NUMBER_OF_EES,
       TERMINATE_AND_CLONE_DATE,
       OPPORTUNITY_TYPE,
        --Is_Product_Multi_Group,
     -- IS_PRODUCT_MULTI_GROUP_DOWNSELL,
       FROM_QUANTITY,
       TO_QUANTITY,
       WTF_SPECIAL_CASE,
       RELEVANT_SPECIAL_CASE,
       IS_RELEVANT_FOR_NUMBER_OF_EES_PER_PRODUCT,
       TEMPORARY_DOWNSELL,
       rn,
       MODEL_TYPE,
       IS_ACTIVE_USAGE_IND,
       END_DATE
      -- carr_flag
-- from DEV_DWH.WATERFALL.FACT_WATERFALL_POP
from prod_DWH.WATERFALL.FACT_WATERFALL_POP

where 1=1
   and COMPANY_ID = '632060'
 and PRODUCT_CODE in ( 'PAY0001')
and MODEL_TYPE = 'actual'
  AND DATE_ID = '2025-08-31'


and REVENUE_TYPE = 'carr'
order by DATE_ID

As a result, the invoice balance remains in the “Prepaid Clearing” GL account, and the automatic journals to move the balance to the prepayment GL account are not being created.  Because the amounts are stuck in the prepaid clearing account, we’re unable to run the prepayment routine. Could you please look into this as a matter of urgency?Steps to Reproduce:Process an invoice with prepayment dates from Zip to NetSuite. Expected Behavior:The invoice should post to GL code 145060 – Prepaid Clearing.An automatic journal should then transfer the balance from GL code 145060 – Prepaid Clearing to GL code 145010 – Prepaid Expenses, creating the corresponding amortisation entry.Actual Behavior:The automatic journal is not being generated in NetSuite to transfer the balance from GL code 145060 – Prepaid Clearing to GL code 145010 – Prepaid Expenses. As a result, the balance remains in the Prepaid Clearing account, and we’re unable to run the prepaid expenses amortisation process.Is there an available workaround?:No———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.description: Email address:noam.muskat@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Billing contact from the wrong partner was added to Anecdotes account, causing incorrect invoice delivery and payment confusionYour Department:FinancePlease specify your team:Billing & CollectionFinance Area:BillingBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:HighRegression:Not SureSystems Involved:SFDCDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:Anecdotes account in SF: https://hibob.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/0017S000005CUfGQAW/viewConni by ananas.codes account in SF: https://hibob.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/0017S00000iF5FMQA0/view1. Invoice INVUS33266 belongs to Anecdotes, but was also sent to Conni by ananas.codes.2. Payment was received from ananas.codes, who claimed the payment was for INVUS33266.3. Upon checking the SF record for Anecdotes, I found two billing contacts, one from Anecdotes and one from ananas.codes.4. In NetSuite system information (see attached screenshot), I saw a pattern of billing contact changes back and forth between both partners.Expected Behavior:Each account should only have billing contacts relevant to their own organization.Invoices should only be sent to the correct customer based on ownership and contact data.Actual Behavior:A billing contact from ananas.codes was added to the Anecdotes account, causing them to receive an invoice that doesn't belong to them and resulting in a mistaken payment.This is a sensitive issue, as it caused real confusion and incorrect payment.I need to understand:How this billing contact was added incorrectlyWhat caused the sync or assignment issueIs there an available workaround?:No———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

Open a PO that was raised in a local currency (other than USD) and has an open amount2. Try multiplying Open Amount/Used Amount by Exchange rate.3. See that the resulting values are different from the actual USD figures.Expected Behavior:USD figures (Open Amount and Used Amount) = Local Currency Figures multiplied by Exchange rate as of the PO date.Actual Behavior:USD Used/Open amounts are both calculated as of the matched Bill date instead of PO date.Is there an available workaround?:NoPlease provide any relevant links:https://4672400.app.netsuite.com/app/common/custom/custrecordentry.nl?rectype=2518&id=1587———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: Invoice INVIL40380 preview shows fixed usage but the customer billed for fixed + usage
bugs.description: Email address:noam.muskat@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Invoice INVIL40380 preview shows fixed usage but the customer billed for fixed + usageYour Department:FinancePlease specify your team:Billing & CollectionFinance Area:BillingBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:HighRegression:YesSystems Involved:NetsuiteDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:1. Open invoice INVIL40380 in NetSuite2. Review the invoice layout and total amount3. Preview previous invoices for the same customer to compareExpected Behavior:The invoice should display both fixed users and usage users but showed only fixed users with the total amount of both fixed and usageActual Behavior:The invoice displays only the Fixed Users line item, but the total amount includes both Fixed and Usage, creating a discrepancy and confusing presentation.Previewing earlier invoices shows the same issue, though invoices sent to the customer in past months appeared correct - suggesting a recent issue impacting usage display, likely affecting IL customers.We need to assess the scope of impact (how many IL customers were affected), identify the root cause of the issue causing usage lines not to display, and determine whether the existing invoices can be corrected and reissued in their original form to ensure accurate documentation for the affected customers.Is there an available workaround?:NoPlease provide any relevant links:https://4672400.app.netsuite.com/app/accounting/transactions/custinvc.nl?id=8311611———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.description: Email address:noam.muskat@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Split customer (94769 – StuRents Limited) displayed as main, blocking contact update to UK Payroll sub-accountYour Department:FinancePlease specify your team:Billing & CollectionFinance Area:BillingBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:HighRegression:Not SureSystems Involved:SFDCDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:Open Salesforce account for customer 94769 – StuRents Limited.Attempt to update the main billing contact via Update Billing Contact.Observe that no sub-entities or related companies are displayed in this section (under payroll customer the main section was filled)Proceed with updating the contact, assuming this is a regular or merged customer.Expected Behavior:For split customers, the system should show sub-entities (e.g., UK Payroll) in both Non Commercial Changes and Update Billing Contact flows, allowing users to select the correct company before applying changesActual Behavior:The customer appeared as a regular or merged entity (no sub-entities visible).Contact update was applied only to the main account.Only after the customer reported the issue did we discover that the account was configured as split, with a hidden sub-account (UK Payroll).In Salesforce, the payroll account was marked as main under the Payroll Customer field.Once that flag was removed and the page refreshed, the sub-entity options became visible, and only then was the contact change successfully applied to the correct UK Payroll sub-account (both in SF and NS).Is there an available workaround?:YesPlease describe the workaround (steps):Check if there is payroll customer, enter this section, unmark the mail fieldPlease provide any relevant links:https://hibob.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/0017S00000W0pg3QAB/view———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: DealHub Additional Conditions section formats basic wording incorrectly
bugs.description:
Please assist with the following instructions provided by DH support : Regarding the issue where the bullet list didn’t display properly after copying rich text from quote HB-120794 into a draft quote, and then previewing the PDF—it’s possible the problem stems from the source quote being created under an older version where G1 was still active.

For our follow-up session, I suggest we ensure everything is done within the same environment that has G2 enabled, and proceed with the following steps:
Activate a version with G2 enabled
We can use the duplicate version (UAT TEST VERSION) created by Guy Snitovsky on our previous call.
Create a new quote
Copy rich text from Salesforce Chatter
Paste it into the quote's 'Deal Desk Additional Conditions' question
Save the quote
Preview the PDF to confirm formatting is retained
Create a second quote
Copy the rich text from the first quote
Paste it again into the second quote’s 'Deal Desk Additional Conditions'
Save
Preview the PDF to confirm results

I also performed these steps in my sandbox environment and confirmed that the formatting was successfully retained.Email address:emma.pearse@hibob.ioIssue Summary:DealHub Additional Conditions section formats basic wording incorrectly Your Department:FinOpsBusiness Priority:MediumBusiness Severity:LowRegression:Not SureSystems Involved:DealhubDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:Ensure when wording is copied from outside DealHub and then pasted into DealHub additional conditions section, the formatting remains as it is outside of DealHub. Expected Behavior:That the above should happen. Actual Behavior:When wording is pasted into DealHub ACs section, the formatting is completely off. The font is too large or differs to other parts of the wording. It looks messy and can't be presented to customers like this. Is there an available workaround?:YesPlease describe the workaround (steps):Time consuming typing long wording out from scratch within DealHub itself. Please provide any relevant links:https://service-us1.dealhub.io/#/quoteGeneration_new?op=TRFf5H2e3W6PEWGo&quoteGUID=1vX89SubkJMltMu8&mode=viewEdit&opSrc=OWNER&opName=CFC%20Underwriting%20PRH%20Upsell%202025&qtName=PRH%20Add%20on%20———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: Production - UK Payroll - Amendment  opportunity displays Free-of-charge line blocking EE reduction
bugs.description: Email address:shahar.reich@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Free-of-charge month in UK Payroll creates an extra contract line, blocking EE reductionYour Department:MISBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:HighRegression:Not SureSystems Involved:DealhubDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:Go to the following Amendment opportunity: Open the quote within the opportunity.Scroll to the section titled "UK Payroll Existing Contract".You’ll see that there are two lines – one for the paid period and one for the free-of-charge (FOC) month, instead of just one.Expected Behavior:Only one contract line should be generated for UK Payroll, even when a free-of-charge month is applied.Actual Behavior:The system creates two lines under "UK Payroll Existing Contract" – one for the paid period and another for the free month.This results in an error when trying to reduce the number of employees:"The total number of EEs from all UK Payroll sites cannot exceed the number of Min EEs specified in the contract."Is there an available workaround?:NoPlease provide any relevant links:https://hibob--dealhub.vf.force.com/apex/DealHub?isdtp=mn&param=CreateQuoteFromOpp&OpportunityId=006Vg00000J3kJWIAZ———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: Integration between Asana and Jira is updating asana to many times
bugs.description: Email address:alex.duhovny@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Integration between Asana and Jira is updating asana to many timesYour Department:MISBusiness Priority:MediumBusiness Severity:MediumRegression:NoSystems Involved:OtherPlease specify:The Jira-Asana integration is generating duplicate automated notifications/comments, flooding the Asana task with redundant nodes, causing confusion and notification overload.Does the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:Open bug ticket via Asana-see exmple:https://app.asana.com/1/103035621276259/project/1205457372216386/task/1210373831431120?focus=trueExpected Behavior:Not to get so many alertsActual Behavior:Multiple Notifications:The Jira-Asana integration is repeatedly posting comments, creating duplicate or repetitive notifications in the Asana task, thus overwhelming users with notifications.Redundant Comments:Specifically, the screenshot shows multiple entries from "Bob Automation," each reporting a comment from Jira back to Asana, essentially creating unnecessary loops or duplicates.Nested Comments:Each automation-generated comment is referencing the original task repeatedly, causing clutter. It appears each action in Jira is reflected multiple times in Asana rather than just once.Is there an available workaround?:NoPlease provide any relevant links:-see exmple: https://app.asana.com/1/103035621276259/project/1205457372216386/task/1210373831431120?focus=true———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259

bugs.summary: Outreach: sequences are stopping for an unknown reason
bugs.description: 2 of my reps, Caitlin and Risha, have been using the APJ | Compensation | call first sequence and have been having multiple issues with the system auto finishing prospects before the first step is started and on a non-finish step (adding on linkedin).I have changed the amount of how many people can be added at a time within 24 hours, and seemed to solve some of the issues. We also got a lot of new call dispositions over the last day or two (and we don't know which ones finish the sequence), so maybe that could have impacted it?Two main issues:Finishing sequence on step 2 (adding on LinkedIn)--they should always still be in sequence post this stepWhen they add people into a sequence, it auto finishes--before the first step is actionableWhen adding people into the sequence, Outreach is auto moving accounts to “nurture/not relevant” and finishing sequence…The account is not in not relevant on SFDCThis needs to be fixed ASAP. Please let me know if you need anything else from our end. Thank you

bugs.summary: Syncing issue in payments between Younium and NS
bugs.description: Email address:krishna.kandasamy@hibob.ioIssue Summary:Syncing issue in payments between Younium and NS Your Department:FinancePlease specify your team:Billing & CollectionFinance Area:BillingBusiness Priority:HighBusiness Severity:HighRegression:YesSystems Involved:NetsuiteDoes the issue relate to an ongoing project (currently in work)?:NoSteps to Reproduce:Payments that are marked as paid in NetSuite are not synced into Younium, and is still showing as outstanding in Younium. Examples:customer Entia paid £72.00 on 11 April (PAYPUK13588) but invoice number INV-3008741 is outstanding in Younium.Starling paid £22374.00 on 23 April (PAYPUK13677) but invoice number INV-3008678 is outstanding in Younium.This issue is still ongoing because payment received yesterday for example from Goodshape (PAYPUK13682) is also not synced to Younium.I manually marked the invoice (INV-3008785) of Gardner Leader as paid in Younium as the customer was complaining regarding the dunning emails. Expected Behavior:NS and Younium invoices should be aligned with paymentsActual Behavior:Payments that are marked as paid in NetSuite are not synced into Younium, and is still showing as outstanding in Younium. Is there an available workaround?:No———————————————This task was submitted through Business Technologies - Production Issuehttps://form.asana.com/?k=RKej9XVfEeD-utH9V4zY7w&d=103035621276259Created from Asana: https://app.asana.com/0/0/1210099716583343/f

bugs.summary: Net price Per Unit Rounding
bugs.description: Detailed requirements:In DealHub, there’s sometimes a mismatch between Net Per Unit price and total Net Price - The issue arises because there’s a mismatch between the value that is being billed via NetSuite and the total Net Price that the client signs off on in the quote. This results in discrepancies between the signed quote in SFDC and the invoiced amount in NetSuite, which can lead to client confusion and billing disputes.in the following example net price per unit = 5.25$ ,quanitty is 1100$ and duration is 4 - while the calcualted total price should be 23100$, the net price on the quote is 23086$ but the billed on netsuite amount is 23100$.please check why this happens on delahub & salesforce and suggest how to fix the discrepency.  \
